items.title: Federal Reserve keeps rates unchanged despite Trump calls
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Federal Reserve pushed the pause button on its interest rate cuts, leaving its key rate unchanged at about 3.6% after lowering it three times last year. #federalreserve #interestrates #jeromepowell

items.title: Ilhan Omar criticizes Trump's 'hateful rhetoric' after attack
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>During a speech in Iowa on Tuesday, shortly before Omar was attacked, the President said immigrants need to be proud of the United States — “not like Ilhan Omar.” (AP Video by Laura Bargfeld) Read more: https://bit.ly/49LX9F7

items.content:
<article>Two federal officers fired shots during the encounter that killed ICU nurse Alex Pretti over the weekend in Minneapolis, a Customs and Border Protection official told Congress in a notice sent Tuesday. (AP Production: Marissa Duhaney) #alexpretti #minneapolis

items.title: Trump sues JPMorgan Chase for $5 billion, AP Explains
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump sued banking giant JPMorgan Chase and its CEO Jamie Dimon for $5 billion over allegations that JPMorgan stopped providing banking services to him and his businesses for political reasons after he left office in January 2021. The lawsuit, filed in Miami-Dade County court in Florida, alleges that JPMorgan abruptly closed multiple accounts in February 2021 with just 60 days notice and no explanation.

(Reposting to correct Trump National Gold Club, LLC to Trump National Golf Club, LLC in graphic at 1:15 seconds)</article>

items.title: 'Wuthering Heights' press junket at an LA mansion
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Follow AP reporter Sarah Jones-Smith into a landmark LA mansion for the "Wuthering Heights" press junket. Emerald Fennell's film starring Margot Robbie and Jacob Elordi hits theaters Feb. 13.
#wutheringheights #MargotRobbie #JacobElordi #EmeraldFennell</article>

items.title: Senators condemn political violence after attack on Ilhan Omar
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Both Republican and Democratic senators condemned any acts of political violence after a man sprayed an unknown substance on Democratic U.S. Rep. Ilhan Omar at a town hall in Minneapolis on Tuesday night.

items.content:

items.content:
<article>Testiyfing before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Secretary of State Marco Rubio downplayed concerns that the Trump administration is undermining NATO, but also repeated a long-standing complaint that allies need to boost defense spending. #NATO #rubio

items.content:
